title: Top Income Shares in the United States, 1913-2020: New Evidence from Tax Records

sections.title: Capital Income at the Very Top
sections.label: sec:capital-top-19

tables.caption: Tax Return Data Summary Statistics, Selected Years
tables.label: tab:tax-data-summary

qa.question: How many tax units were there in 2019?
qa.answer: 155.8 million

qa.question: What was the top 1% AGI threshold in 2019?

tables.caption: Appendix: Tax Unit Adjustments for Changing Filing Patterns
tables.label: tab:appendix-tax-units

qa.question: Has the fraction of married-filing-jointly returns changed since 1950?
qa.answer: Yes, declined from 74.4% to 48.2%, reflecting rising rates of single filing

tables.caption: Effect of Capital Gains on Top Income Shares
tables.label: tab:capital-gains-inclusion

qa.question: How much higher is the top 1% share under mark-to-market vs excluding capital gains in 2019?
qa.answer: 6.2 percentage points higher (23.4% vs 17.2%)

qa.question: Why is 2007 an outlier in the realized CG series?
qa.answer: High realized capital gains in 2007 before the financial crisis created a spike in top income shares

tables.caption: AGI Adjustments: Effect of Including Excluded Income Sources
tables.label: tab:agi-adjustments

qa.question: What is the top 1% share under the comprehensive income definition in 2019?
Does including transfer income increase or decrease the top 1% share?
qa.answer: Decrease: from 18.8% baseline to 16.4% with transfers, as transfers mainly benefit lower-income groups

qa.question: What was the top 0.1% share in the 1920s?
When was the top 0.1% share at its lowest?
What was the top 1% income share in the 2010s?
What was the top 1% share in the 1970s (the low point)?
What was the top 10% income share in the 2010s?
What fraction of the top 10% share belongs to the top 1%?
qa.answer: Approximately 39.7% (18.74/47.21) in the 2010s

tables.caption: Pareto Coefficients at the Top of the Income Distribution, Selected Years
tables.label: tab:pareto-coefficients-by-year

qa.question: What does a lower Pareto alpha indicate?
qa.answer: Heavier tails and greater inequality at the top

qa.question: Is the 2019 Pareto alpha above or below 2?
qa.answer: Below 2 (1.692 at the top 1% threshold), indicating potentially infinite variance

tables.caption: Gini Coefficient Time Series, United States, 1913-2019
tables.label: tab:gini-coefficient-time-series

qa.question: What was the pre-tax Gini coefficient in 2019?
Is wealth more unequally distributed than income?
qa.answer: Yes, the wealth Gini (0.869) is substantially higher than the income Gini (0.582) in 2019

tables.caption: Composition of Top Incomes: Wages vs. Capital, Selected Years
tables.label: tab:composition-wages-vs-capital

qa.question: Has the labor income share of the top 1% increased or decreased since 1950?
qa.answer: Increased: from 38.4% in 1950 to 54.8% in 2019

qa.question: What happened to the dividend share of top 1% income from 1950 to 2019?
qa.answer: Declined substantially, from 24.1% to 8.7%

qa.question: What was the mean income of the top 0.1% in 2019?
qa.answer: $5.218 million

qa.question: How does wage share vary across the top of the distribution?
qa.answer: Decreasing: 73.4% at P90-P95 to 34.8% at the top 0.1%

tables.caption: Gender Decomposition of Top Income Shares, United States
tables.label: tab:gender-decomposition

qa.question: What share of top 0.1% income did women earn in 2019?
Has women's representation at the top grown over time?
qa.answer: Yes, consistently increasing at all percentile groups from 1980 to 2019

tables.caption: Industry Decomposition of Top 1\% Income, 2000 and 2019 (\%)
tables.label: tab:industry-decomposition

qa.question: Which sector has the highest representation in the top 1% in 2019?
qa.answer: Finance and insurance (24.1%)

qa.question: Which sector grew most in top 1% share from 2000 to 2019?
qa.answer: Technology/information, rising 5.6 percentage points

tables.caption: Cross-Country Comparison of Top 1\% Income Shares (\%)
tables.label: tab:cross-country-comparison

qa.question: Which country had the highest top 1% share in 2019?
qa.answer: United States (18.8%)

qa.question: Which country has the lowest top 1% share throughout the sample?
qa.answer: Sweden, with shares consistently below 9%

tables.caption: Average Effective Tax Rates by Income Group, Selected Years (\%)
tables.label: tab:tax-rate-changes

qa.question: What was the top 0.01% effective tax rate in 1960?
How much has the top 0.1% effective tax rate changed from 1960 to 2019?
qa.answer: Declined from 62.8% to 30.4%, a reduction of 32.4 percentage points

tables.caption: Wealth vs. Income Top Shares: United States, 1913-2019
tables.label: tab:wealth-vs-income-top-shares

qa.question: What was the top 1% wealth share in 2019?
Is wealth more concentrated than income at the top?
qa.answer: Yes, top 1% wealth share (38.1%) exceeds income share (18.8%) in 2019

tables.caption: Appendix: Price Deflators Used for Income Series
tables.label: tab:appendix-price-deflators

qa.question: Does the choice of price deflator affect estimated top income shares?
qa.answer: No, income shares are ratios and are deflator-invariant (top 1% share ranges only 18.7-18.9% in 2019 across deflators)

tables.caption: Estate Tax Returns: Wealth Concentration Estimates
tables.label: tab:estate-tax-data

qa.question: Why did the number of taxable estates fall sharply from 2000 to 2010?
qa.answer: The 2001 EGTRRA raised the filing threshold from $675k to $1M then to $5M, removing most estates

qa.question: What was the top 0.1% wealth share estimated from estate tax data in 2019?

equations.label: eq:pareto-interp
equations.description: Pareto interpolation between tax brackets to estimate quantile income

qa.question: What is the Pareto interpolation formula?
qa.answer: y_p = y_k * (n_k / (n_k - n*p))^{1/alpha_k}, where alpha_k is estimated from adjacent bracket counts and thresholds

equations.label: eq:pareto
equations.description: Pareto distribution: upper tail probability for income above threshold y_min

qa.question: What is the Pareto distribution?
qa.answer: P(Y > y) = (y_min/y)^alpha for y >= y_min, where alpha > 1 is the Pareto coefficient

equations.label: eq:top-share
equations.latex: S(p) = \frac{\int_{Q(p)}^\infty y \, dF(y)}{\int_0^\infty y \, dF(y)} = 1 - L(p), \quad L(p) = \frac{1}{\mu}\int_0^p Q(u)\,du, \quad \frac{\partial L(p)}{\partial p} = \frac{Q(p)}{\mu}
equations.description: Top income share formula: mean income above p-th quantile times share of population

qa.question: How is the top p income share computed?
qa.answer: S(p) = mean income above the p-th quantile times (1-p), divided by overall mean income

equations.label: eq:gini-pareto
equations.description: Gini coefficient as a function of the Pareto alpha

qa.question: What is the Gini coefficient for a Pareto distribution with alpha = 2?

equations.label: eq:capital-share-top
equations.latex: \\kappa(p) = \\frac{\\int_{Q(p)}^\\infty y^K \\, dF(y)}{\\int_{Q(p)}^\\infty y \\, dF(y)}, \\quad \\frac{\\partial \\kappa(p)}{\\partial p} = \\frac{Q^K(p) - \\kappa(p) Q(p)}{\\int_{Q(p)}^\\infty y\\,dF(y)}
equations.description: Capital income share within the top p percent group

qa.question: How is the capital share of top incomes defined?
qa.answer: kappa(p) = mean capital income above the p-th quantile divided by mean total income above the p-th quantile

equations.label: eq:pareto-est-19
equations.description: Pareto coefficient estimation from adjacent bracket counts

equations.label: eq:pareto-mean-19
equations.description: Mean income above threshold under Pareto assumption

equations.label: eq:gini-theil-19
equations.description: Gini and Theil inequality measures as functions of Pareto alpha

qa.question: What was the top 1% income share in 2019?
When was the top 0.1% income share at its lowest?
What compositional shift has occurred in top incomes since the 1950s?
qa.answer: A shift from capital income (dividends, interest) to wage and salary income -- the rise of the working rich

qa.question: What has happened to the Pareto alpha since 1970?
qa.answer: Declined from 2.381 in 1970 to 1.692 in 2019, indicating heavier tails and more inequality
